description_long: This is a taxidermy mount of a wild boar's head, featuring a broad, muscular snout, coarse dark gray fur, and prominent tusks protruding from the lower jaw. The ears are upright and slightly tattered, adding to the rugged appearance. The eyes are set deep and forward-facing, giving the animal a focused, alert expression. The fur texture is rough and bristly, with subtle color variations from dark gray to brownish hues. The back of the mount is flat and unfinished, indicating it is designed to be displayed on a wall. The overall form is robust and lifelike, capturing the distinctive features of a wild boar.
description: A lifelike wild boar head mount with coarse dark gray fur, prominent tusks, and upright ears. The back is flat for wall display.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad snout, both tusks, and symmetrical upright ears.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the snout, tusks, and ear shape.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, unfinished back of the mount.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the other profile, ear, and tusk details.
attribute_location: wall mount (urban interior or trophy room)
